title: Gate on fast-tier p99 and standard-tier p95
preview: Summary: This PR adds tier-aware SLO gating to perf-canary for the Workload-aware Batching Defaults launch. We now fail the perf-canary run if: (1) FAST tier p99 latency regresses beyond threshold (tail-latency protection gate), or (2) STANDARD tier p95 latency regresses beyond threshold (steady-state gate). The int...
text: Summary: This PR adds tier-aware SLO gating to perf-canary for the Workload-aware Batching Defaults launch. We now fail the perf-canary run if: (1) FAST tier p99 latency regresses beyond threshold (tail-latency protection gate), or (2) STANDARD tier p95 latency regresses beyond threshold (steady-state gate). The intent is to stop merges that silently violate the published tier SLOs while still allowing legitimate improvements to land with a controlled baseline refresh process. Motivation: As we move batching config from ad-hoc per-route knobs to three customer-facing tiers (fast / standard / cost-efficient), we need CI guardrails that match what we’ll ship. Previously, canary gating was based on a single aggregate p95 and a single scenario per model. That missed fast-tier tail regressions (p99) and could overreact to noise in standard tier when concurrency ramps changed. What changed ...

title: Update release notes for runtime 1.22.0 (FP8/INT8 perf sprint)
preview: Summary: Finalize the runtime 1.22.0 release notes for the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint outcomes. This PR consolidates the kernel + scheduler changes that shipped behind flags, documents the rollout/canary plan and rollback triggers, and adds explicit guardrails/limitations discovered during the sprint (shape...
text: Summary: Finalize the runtime 1.22.0 release notes for the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint outcomes. This PR consolidates the kernel + scheduler changes that shipped behind flags, documents the rollout/canary plan and rollback triggers, and adds explicit guardrails/limitations discovered during the sprint (shape regimes, long-context caveats, and per-region canary learnings). It also aligns wording with Perf Transparency guidance so the same notes can be used internally (release channel) without accidentally making externally-unapproved benchmark claims. Context: This release packages the work from ENG-7422/7423/7424/7425/7426/7427/7430/7431 including (a) FP8 attention kernel integration behind flags, (b) INT8 matmul dispatch + shape guardrails and fallback behavior, (c) kernel auto-selection heuristics for H100/A100 with safe fallbacks, (d) scheduler knob defaults for mixed prefi...

title: Emit reason codes + tracing for workload-aware batching profile selection
preview: Summary: This PR adds explicit reason codes for workload-aware batching profile selection and wires them into structured metrics + tracing so we can answer “what profile did we pick and why?” during canary + rollout. Motivation: As we introduce SLO-tier defaults (fast/standard/cost-efficient) we need explainability ...
text: Summary: This PR adds explicit reason codes for workload-aware batching profile selection and wires them into structured metrics + tracing so we can answer “what profile did we pick and why?” during canary + rollout. Motivation: As we introduce SLO-tier defaults (fast/standard/cost-efficient) we need explainability for operators and for our own regression triage. Today, selection is implicit in scheduler logs and hard to aggregate. This change emits a small, bounded set of reason codes and attaches them to a low-cardinality metric and to a trace span so that we can (a) break down latency/cost by effective profile and (b) understand which policy/constraint caused the selection without parsing logs. Scope: runtime scheduler decision instrumentation only (no policy logic changes, no knob changes). Reason codes are emitted at the final “effective profile” boundary after overrides + constr...

title: Fix race in rollout step transitions that can double-execute rollback
preview: Context: While wiring SLO-driven auto actions into the rollout controller, we observed occasional duplicate rollback execution in staging when the controller received concurrent signals (SLO evaluator tick + manual pause/rollback request, or duplicate queue deliveries). The symptom was two rollback executors attempt...
text: Context: While wiring SLO-driven auto actions into the rollout controller, we observed occasional duplicate rollback execution in staging when the controller received concurrent signals (SLO evaluator tick + manual pause/rollback request, or duplicate queue deliveries). The symptom was two rollback executors attempting to revert the same rollout step, producing: (a) duplicate audit events, (b) traffic split “thrash” (two sequences of step-downs), and (c) occasional terminal state mismatch (rollout marked ROLLED_BACK while the step transition record was still in PROGRESS). Root cause: Step transitions were not guarded by a single-writer constraint. The controller’s state machine allowed two goroutines to pass the precondition checks ("step is eligible to transition") before either persisted the new step state, because step status and rollout version were read outside a transaction and ...
links: Linear: ENG-4829 (rollback executor idempotency and safety)
Linear: ENG-4821 (rollout controller SLO evaluator hook)

title: E2E (staging): simulate SLO breaches and verify auto-pause/rollback + audit/Console surfaces
preview:
What
Adds end-to-end coverage in staging for the new auto-mitigation path (SLO breach -> controller decision -> action execution -> audit trail -> Console surfaces). These tests are intended to gate staged enablement and catch regressions in integration points (monitor adapter, policy engine, controller state machin...
text:
What
Adds end-to-end coverage in staging for the new auto-mitigation path (SLO breach -> controller decision -> action execution -> audit trail -> Console surfaces). These tests are intended to gate staged enablement and catch regressions in integration points (monitor adapter, policy engine, controller state machine, audit event emission, Console API/UI).

Why
We’ve had multiple staging runs where unit/integration tests passed but the real system failed to auto-mitigate due to timing/labeling issues (monitor staleness, wrong rollout scope labels, eventual consistency between controller and Console read model). This PR adds deterministic(ish) staging E2E that:
- injects synthetic latency + error signals for a specific rollout scope (route/model_version/region/tier)
- waits for SLO evaluator to observe a breach (fast window) with minimum sample gating
- asserts controller pauses at the...

title: Post rollout auto-rollback events to Slack (concise notification + links)
preview: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, ...
text: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, (2) the rollout SLO dashboard, and (3) the audit event details. Motivation: during the 2026-02 simulations and recent customer escalations, oncall often learned about auto-mitigations only after secondary alerts or customer reports; this closes the loop and reduces time-to-context while keeping noise bounded. Event source: consumes the new structured audit event emitted to the event stream (redwood PR-28435) with event_type=rollout.auto_action.*. The handler maps event subtypes to Slack severity and routing without paging by default (notify-only) to avoid paging storms. Mes...

title: Add Cost Model API Grafana dashboards (latency/errors, schema version dist, drift)
preview: Summary: Adds a Grafana folder + dashboards for the Cost Model API so Console/Optimize rollouts have a single place to monitor health, adoption, and correctness drift. Includes panels for request volume, p50/p95/p99 latency, error-rate by code, schema version distribution, request-bounds rejections, cache hit/miss (...
text: Summary: Adds a Grafana folder + dashboards for the Cost Model API so Console/Optimize rollouts have a single place to monitor health, adoption, and correctness drift. Includes panels for request volume, p50/p95/p99 latency, error-rate by code, schema version distribution, request-bounds rejections, cache hit/miss (client-side + service memoization), and a first pass of drift metrics (predicted vs observed) sourced from the scheduled drift job. Motivation: During early Console integration testing we saw two recurring issues: (1) latency spikes due to cardinality blowups on hot paths and (2) drift alerts firing due to telemetry lag vs genuine mismatch. This PR standardizes the dashboards and bakes in guardrail signals (telemetry freshness, missing inputs) so oncall can quickly triage. Related: redwood/pr-28457 (service endpoints), redwood/pr-28490 (drift monitoring job), redwood/pr-284...

title: Telemetry: add normalized batch efficiency + queue-time metrics for Cost Model inputs
preview:
Summary
This PR adds and normalizes batch efficiency and queue-time metrics in the telemetry pipeline so Cost Model API v1 can consume stable, low-cardinality, versioned inputs across Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Motivation / Context
The Standard Cost Model project requires consistent inputs for: (1) batch efficiency ...
text:
Summary
This PR adds and normalizes batch efficiency and queue-time metrics in the telemetry pipeline so Cost Model API v1 can consume stable, low-cardinality, versioned inputs across Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Motivation / Context
The Standard Cost Model project requires consistent inputs for: (1) batch efficiency (how effectively requests share a batch over time) and (2) queueing delay (scheduler wait) to accurately attribute request cost and explain cost deltas in Console/Optimize. Today, runtime emits a mix of per-request and per-batch stats with inconsistent naming/units, and the ingestion layer normalizes only a subset. This creates drift across billing hooks vs Console breakdowns and makes Optimize what-if projections hard to ground.

What’s included (high level)
- Adds a canonical set of metrics (names + units + tags) for batch efficiency and queue-time.
- Normalizes legacy me...

title: Perf canary: gate new kernels by model + region with auto-rollback on threshold breach
preview: Motivation: We need to safely roll out FP8/INT8 kernel paths without globally flipping flags. During the sprint we saw model- and region-specific perf deltas (plus noisy samples in low-traffic regions) that make coarse-grained gating too risky. This PR adds per-(model, region) cohort gating for kernel modes, with au...
text:
Motivation: We need to safely roll out FP8/INT8 kernel paths without globally flipping flags. During the sprint we saw model- and region-specific perf deltas (plus noisy samples in low-traffic regions) that make coarse-grained gating too risky. This PR adds per-(model, region) cohort gating for kernel modes, with automatic rollback when TTFT/TPOT breach configured thresholds over a stable sample window.

What this changes (high level): 1) Adds a cohort-aware gate that evaluates perf canary metrics keyed by {model_id, region, gpu_sku, kernel_mode}. 2) Introduces a config-driven rollout plan format that allows staged enablement by cohort (e.g., start with us-east for top-2 models on H100, then expand). 3) Implements auto-rollback: if a cohort breaches threshold for N consecutive evaluation windows (with minimum sample constraints), the controller flips the cohort back to baseline and em...

title: hotfix(runtime): disable FP8 attention kernel for small-q / mid-kv shape regime (canary TTFT regression)
preview: Context: Perf canary flagged a p95 TTFT regression after enabling the FP8 attention kernel in eu-west for a subset of traffic. The regression is concentrated in a specific shape regime where the FP8 attention kernel launch/epilogue overhead dominates (small q_len decode steps + mid-range kv_len), leading to worse en...
text:
Context: Perf canary flagged a p95 TTFT regression after enabling the FP8 attention kernel in eu-west for a subset of traffic. The regression is concentrated in a specific shape regime where the FP8 attention kernel launch/epilogue overhead dominates (small q_len decode steps + mid-range kv_len), leading to worse end-to-end TTFT despite kernel-level wins in other regimes.

This PR is a safety hotfix for 1.22.0: add a dispatch guard that disables FP8 attention for the known-bad shape regime and falls back to the baseline (BF16/FP16) attention kernel path. This is intended to unblock canary re-enable for the rest of the population while we root-cause and optimize the FP8 kernel for this regime.

Regime disabled (attention-only):
- q_len <= 16 (decode micro-steps / tool-calling structured-output workloads)
- 768 <= kv_len <= 3072 (mid-context; observed worst around ~2k)
- head_dim == 128...

title: Cost Model API: add policy guardrails for what-if projections (bounds + allowed ranges)
preview: Context: The new Cost Model what-if endpoint is intentionally flexible (batching/caching/quantization knobs) and will be called by Optimize (interactive) and potentially Console (hot paths). In early staging tests and after SUP-31877 triage, we saw two failure modes: (1) callers passing extreme parameter values (e.g...
text: Context: The new Cost Model what-if endpoint is intentionally flexible (batching/caching/quantization knobs) and will be called by Optimize (interactive) and potentially Console (hot paths). In early staging tests and after SUP-31877 triage, we saw two failure modes: (1) callers passing extreme parameter values (e.g., batch size 4096, cache warm-rate > 1.0) leading to unrealistic savings outputs, and (2) expensive computations when callers submit high-cardinality workload summaries (large histograms / too many route keys) that can blow our latency budget and increase tail p95s. This PR adds explicit policy guardrails at the API boundary so the service is safe-by-default, deterministic, and bounded. Summary of changes: (A) Introduce a centralized PolicyGuardrails layer for what-if requests that validates and clamps input ranges (where clamping is explicitly allowed) and rejects invalid...

title: Add FP8/INT8 benchmark + rollout Grafana dashboards and canary alerting

This PR adds a small dashboard pack + alert rules in `observability-pack`:

text:
## Context
Serving Runtime is running an FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking + staged rollout sprint. SRE/Runtime asked for a single place to monitor benchmark runs (offline) and the perf-canary rollout (online) with consistent dimensions.

This PR adds a small dashboard pack + alert rules in `observability-pack`:
- Grafana dashboards for benchmark runs and rollout monitoring
- Prometheus recording rules to normalize TTFT/TPOT/tokens/s aggregations and reduce query cost
- Alert rules intended for perf canary gating (and incident-style triage)

## What’s included
### 1) Dashboard: **FP8/INT8 – Benchmark Runs**
Folder: `Serving Runtime / Performance`
Primary purpose: benchmark execution monitoring + sanity checks while running the matrix.
Panels:
- TTFT (ms) p50/p95 by `model`, `gpu_sku`, `kernel_mode` (baseline|fp8|int8), `region`
- TPOT (ms/token) p50/p95 (decode-heavy visibility)
- Tokens/s...

title: Optimize scheduler knob defaults for mixed prefill/decode workloads
preview:
Title: Optimize scheduler knob defaults for mixed prefill/decode workloads

Context
During the FP8/INT8 benchmarking sprint we saw several cases where microbench wins (kernel runtime) did not translate cleanly to end-to-end improvements in the hosted runtime under mixed workloads (concurrent requests with a realisti...
text:
Title: Optimize scheduler knob defaults for mixed prefill/decode workloads

Context
During the FP8/INT8 benchmarking sprint we saw several cases where microbench wins (kernel runtime) did not translate cleanly to end-to-end improvements in the hosted runtime under mixed workloads (concurrent requests with a realistic prefill/decode mix). The main failure mode was scheduler-induced head-of-line effects: decode-heavy phases could keep the GPU busy, but TTFT (and TTFT p95 in particular) regressed because prefill admission and batching windows were tuned for "throughput-first" steady-state rather than mixed interactive traffic.

This PR updates the default scheduler knob set for the "mixed" workload profile to better realize kernel wins without paying a TTFT tail penalty. Changes were validated against the v3 top-hosted benchmark suite and compared to baseline scheduler defaults.

What ch...

title: Metrics: tag runtime perf histograms by model/GPU/kernel-mode for canary debugging
preview:
Context
We’re rolling out FP8/INT8 kernel paths behind flags and gating via perf canary. Today, the runtime exports TTFT/TPOT/tokens-per-sec histograms without enough context to quickly localize regressions. During the last canary rollback we had to correlate node labels + routing logs by hand to infer which model/G...
text:
Context
We’re rolling out FP8/INT8 kernel paths behind flags and gating via perf canary. Today, the runtime exports TTFT/TPOT/tokens-per-sec histograms without enough context to quickly localize regressions. During the last canary rollback we had to correlate node labels + routing logs by hand to infer which model/GPU/mode cohort was affected.

What
This PR adds low-cardinality dimensions to a small set of runtime perf metrics so canary comparisons can be sliced directly by:
- model_id (stable registry identifier; not the human display name)
- gpu_sku (coarse SKU string, e.g., H100-SXM/A100-80GB/L4)
- kernel_mode (baseline|fp8|int8)

Key points / guardrails
- Cardinality: model_id is allowlisted to “top hosted models” (config-driven); all other models emit model_id="other". gpu_sku is derived from the node’s GPU SKU label, normalized to a small set. kernel_mode is an enum from the ker...

title: Audit log pipeline dashboards + alerts (ingestion latency, drop rate) with runbook links
preview:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR a...
text:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR adds:
- A Grafana dashboard for the audit-log ingestion pipeline (end-to-end delivery latency, ingest backlog, drop rate, retry/429/5xx, DLQ growth).
- Alert rules for delivery latency and drop rate with sane defaults + per-environment routing.
- Runbook links embedded in alert annotations and dashboard panel descriptions.

Key signals / definitions:
- delivery_latency_seconds: time from event emission at producer to ack at ingestion sink (measured via audit-ingest gateway metrics + exporter timestamps).
- drop_rate: fraction of events not successfully delivered after retry ...

title: Add TTFT/TPOT threshold profiles for FP8/INT8 kernel canaries (per-SKU baselines + noise suppression)
preview: Context: As part of the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint, we’re enabling quantized kernel modes behind flags and rolling out via perf canary. The existing canary gating uses a single threshold policy that was tuned on baseline (FP16/BF16) runs and has been noisy for quantized modes (especially when sample sizes a...
text: Context: As part of the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint, we’re enabling quantized kernel modes behind flags and rolling out via perf canary. The existing canary gating uses a single threshold policy that was tuned on baseline (FP16/BF16) runs and has been noisy for quantized modes (especially when sample sizes are small or when the scheduler mix shifts TTFT/TPOT differently). This PR introduces explicit threshold profiles for quantized kernels and per-SKU baselines so we can gate safely and reduce false positives while still catching real regressions. Summary of changes: (1) Add threshold profiles keyed by kernel_mode (baseline|fp8|int8) and sku (h100|a100|l4) with separate TTFT/TPOT constraints and minimum sample requirements. (2) Add noise suppression: a small-sample guardrail, robust aggregation using trimmed mean for p95 deltas, and an EWMA-based stability check that prevents ...

title: docs(runtime): document FP8/INT8 kernel flag names + safe rollout playbook links
preview:
Context
We’ve accumulated a few FP8/INT8-related flags across the sprint (kernel integration, autoselect heuristics, and perf-canary gating). During the kickoff and variance triage we had repeated questions in Slack about:

text:
Context
We’ve accumulated a few FP8/INT8-related flags across the sprint (kernel integration, autoselect heuristics, and perf-canary gating). During the kickoff and variance triage we had repeated questions in Slack about:
- the canonical flag names (runtime vs runner vs perf-canary)
- which flags are safe to toggle in production vs benchmark-only
- how to stage enablement to avoid customer-impacting regressions

This PR updates internal runtime docs + flag comments so engineers and SREs can quickly find the correct knobs and the safe rollout procedure.

What’s in this PR
1) Canonical flag names + semantics (internal)
- Documented the runtime-level flags used for the FP8/INT8 sprint and clarified naming conventions.
- Added explicit “DO NOT USE in prod” notes for benchmark-only toggles.

Key flags (as documented)
- runtime.kernel_mode
 - Values: baseline | fp8 | int8 | auto
 - Notes: ...

title: Console: switch cost breakdown aggregation to Cost Model API (flagged + fallback + metrics)
preview: Problem: Console cost breakdown pages currently rely on legacy aggregation logic (token metering + heuristics) that can diverge from billing hooks and Optimize. This PR updates Console backend aggregation to call the internal Cost Model API v1 for per-request and aggregated breakdowns, behind a feature flag, with ex...
text: Problem: Console cost breakdown pages currently rely on legacy aggregation logic (token metering + heuristics) that can diverge from billing hooks and Optimize. This PR updates Console backend aggregation to call the internal Cost Model API v1 for per-request and aggregated breakdowns, behind a feature flag, with explicit fail-open fallback to the legacy path to protect the Console hot path. Scope: (1) New CostModelClient wiring in console-backend using the shared client library (timeouts/retries/version negotiation), (2) route-level and org-level breakdown queries now source canonical taxonomy from the cost-model service (prefill/decode, cache hits, batch efficiency, overhead allocation), (3) feature flag + gradual rollout controls, (4) metrics/tracing so we can compare legacy vs cost-model output and watch latency/error budgets. Flagging/rollout: gated by `console.cost_breakdown.use...

title: Deploy gate (warn-only): surface error budget burn in CI/CD + Slack notify
preview: Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget enforcement rollout (Q1 2026), we need an initial “warn-only” phase that makes error budget state visible at deploy-time without blocking critical fixes. This PR adds a deploy gate step to our CI/CD pipeline that queries the current error budget state for the target ...
text: Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget enforcement rollout (Q1 2026), we need an initial “warn-only” phase that makes error budget state visible at deploy-time without blocking critical fixes. This PR adds a deploy gate step to our CI/CD pipeline that queries the current error budget state for the target tier/region, posts a warning to Slack when a tier is in a red/burning state, and logs any operator override intent (even though warn-only does not block). Scope: Hosted + Dedicated tiers; prod deploys only; Private out of scope. Behavior: (1) Determine deploy context (tier, region, env) from the deployment manifest and pipeline metadata. (2) Fetch error budget status from the SLO service (slo-control-plane) using the new `/v1/error-budget/state` endpoint (rolling 30d window, plus burn-rate annotations). (3) If state is `burning` or `exhausted`, emit a CI annotation + Slack ...

title: Post error budget burn summaries to #eng-sre on burn-rate alerts (dashboards + runbook links)
preview: Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget program, we’re standardizing burn-rate alerting and enforcement. Today the burn-rate alerts page the on-call, but triage is still slow because the initial signal doesn’t include (a) how fast we’re burning, (b) what’s driving it (latency vs errors vs streaming disconn...
text:
Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget program, we’re standardizing burn-rate alerting and enforcement. Today the burn-rate alerts page the on-call, but triage is still slow because the initial signal doesn’t include (a) how fast we’re burning, (b) what’s driving it (latency vs errors vs streaming disconnects), or (c) where to go next (dashboards/runbooks). This PR updates incident-bot to listen for burn-rate alert notifications and post a structured “burn summary” message into #eng-sre (and thread replies for updates), including deep links to the tiered SLO dashboards and the active mitigation runbook.

What this does: 1) Adds a new alert type handler for error budget burn-rate alerts (Prometheus Alertmanager payloads via our existing webhook). 2) Builds a canonical Slack message format: header with SLO name + tier + region, a short diagnosis (burn rate + window + budget r...

title: Add audit log lag dashboard + alerts and wire runbook links (ENG-4824)
preview: Context: Support + CS escalations (notably around audit log delivery latency) highlighted that we lacked a first-class, Support/CS-friendly view of end-to-end audit log pipeline lag and an alert that aligns to enterprise expectations. This PR adds a dedicated dashboard and alerting rules for audit log lag, and ensur...
text:
Context: Support + CS escalations (notably around audit log delivery latency) highlighted that we lacked a first-class, Support/CS-friendly view of end-to-end audit log pipeline lag and an alert that aligns to enterprise expectations. This PR adds a dedicated dashboard and alerting rules for audit log lag, and ensures panels/alerts link to a canonical runbook. It is intended to reduce time-to-triage during tickets like SUP-1846 and to support the known-issues sprint playbook work by providing an authoritative signal.

What’s included: (1) New Grafana dashboard: "Audit Logs — Delivery Lag" with panels for ingestion → transform → delivery stages, p50/p95/p99 lag, backlog depth (where available), per-region breakdown, and a quick "is this customer-impacting" section (current lag vs expected SLO). (2) New Prometheus alerts: audit-log-lag warning + critical, scoped to production namespaces...

title: Prompt for + pin related SUP tickets and customer-impact summary in incident bridge channels
preview: Context: Support/SRE asked for a consistent way to keep customer escalations discoverable after a bridge ends. Today, bridge channels frequently miss links to the SUP ticket(s), and the customer-impact summary gets repeated across Slack/Jira/email with drift. This PR updates incident-bot to (1) prompt the bridge cre...
text: Context: Support/SRE asked for a consistent way to keep customer escalations discoverable after a bridge ends. Today, bridge channels frequently miss links to the SUP ticket(s), and the customer-impact summary gets repeated across Slack/Jira/email with drift. This PR updates incident-bot to (1) prompt the bridge creator for related SUP tickets and a customer-impact summary, (2) post and pin a single "Escalation context" message in the bridge channel, and (3) keep the channel topic synced with SUP links (without including customer names). Behavior: - When a bridge channel is created via the bot (or when the bot detects it has been invited to a newly created bridge channel), the bot posts a message with two required actions: "Add related Jira tickets" (accepts SUP-* and optionally INT-*), and "Add customer impact" (free text w/ guidance). - Once submitted, the bot edits the message into...

title: Improve streaming disconnect errors (request ID propagation + clearer classification)
preview: Context: Support escalations around streaming resets/truncation (notably us-east) have been slowed by ambiguous client-visible errors and missing request IDs in some disconnect paths. This PR improves server-side error classification for streaming disconnects and ensures request ID propagation is consistent across g...
text: Context: Support escalations around streaming resets/truncation (notably us-east) have been slowed by ambiguous client-visible errors and missing request IDs in some disconnect paths. This PR improves server-side error classification for streaming disconnects and ensures request ID propagation is consistent across gateway -> runtime -> client responses/logs. The goal is to let Support quickly distinguish (a) client/proxy disconnects, (b) upstream runtime cancellations, and (c) internal gateway timeouts, and to reliably hand customers a request_id they can paste back into tickets. Changes: (1) Introduce a dedicated StreamingDisconnect error category with stable public error codes and safe-to-share messaging. (2) Guarantee request_id inclusion in error payloads for streaming responses (including mid-stream termination) and in trailing metadata where applicable. (3) Improve logging: emit...

title: Add shared Cost Model API client library (timeouts, retries, schema negotiation)
preview:
Context
Console and Optimize are both adopting the Cost Model API (see ENG-4821/4822). We need a single, opinionated client that standardizes:
- request timeouts (hot path safety for Console)
- retries (bounded + only when safe)
- schema version negotiation (so callers can be forward-compatible and we can roll schem...
text:
Context
Console and Optimize are both adopting the Cost Model API (see ENG-4821/4822). We need a single, opinionated client that standardizes:
- request timeouts (hot path safety for Console)
- retries (bounded + only when safe)
- schema version negotiation (so callers can be forward-compatible and we can roll schema changes safely)
- consistent auth/tenancy + tracing/metrics

What this PR does
- Adds a shared client library for service-to-service calls to Cost Model API:
 - package: //go/platform/costmodel/client (Go)
 - exported type: costmodel.Client
 - supports endpoints: EstimateCost, GetCostBreakdown, WhatIf
- Timeouts
 - default request timeout: 250ms (overridable per-call)
 - default connect timeout: 50ms
 - caller can pass a context with deadline; client will respect the stricter of (ctx deadline, configured timeout)
- Retries
 - retries are bounded: max 2 retries, exponentia...

title: perf-canary: add paging (Sev2/Sev1) alert rules for sustained per-model regressions
preview: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Mot...
text: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Motivation: During phase-1 rollout we saw warning-level regressions that were actionable when they sustained (real tail-latency impact), but the existing rules were designed to be noisy-tolerant and non-paging. This PR implements the paging path that SRE approved in the paging policy thread and is required for rollout gates integration. Alerting model: We page only for top-model + sustained regressions where we have enough canary volume to reduce false positives, and we gate on a canary-health signal (telemetry freshness + request loop healthy) to avoid paging on broken canary...

title: Add incident taxonomy dashboards + alerting (type/owner breakdowns + playbook links)

## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

text:
## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

Key deliverables in this PR:
- Grafana dashboard: **Incident Taxonomy (v1)**
 - incident volume and impact proxy panels (by tier, severity)
 - breakdowns by **incident_type** and **owner_team**
 - classification compliance (% unknown/unclassified)
 - MTTA/MTTR approximations using incident event timestamps
 - drilldowns from type -> owner -> recent incidents (table)
 - playbook links based on incident_type (panel links + table column)
- Prometheus rules:
 - recording rules to normalize/aggregate incident event counters into a stable set of labels
 - alert rules for (1) unclassified rate re...

title: perf-canary: add warning-level alert rules (dampening + min sample size) routed to #eng-runtime
preview:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sampl...
text:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sample conditions
- Alertmanager routing to Slack #eng-runtime
- Runbook + dashboard links in annotations

This is intended to be enabled first in us-east with a limited model list (per ENG-4880 rollout plan). Paging rules are tracked separately (pr-19183).

## Motivation / context
During early perf-canary rollout we saw false positives driven by low volume, baseline drift, and transient telemetry delays. The warning rules in this PR aim to be:
- Low-noise: only fire when there’s enough data
- Actionable: include baseline vs observed deltas + direct links
- Safe: warning-only ch...

title: Runtime: KV compaction kill-switch + config plumbing (oncall-safe disable path)
preview: Context: KV cache compaction/eviction is rolling out behind flags. During canaries we need a deterministic, oncall-friendly way to disable compaction immediately (or gate it to a safe minimum) without requiring a full runtime restart, while preserving allocator invariants and avoiding relocation-in-progress hazards....
text:
Context: KV cache compaction/eviction is rolling out behind flags. During canaries we need a deterministic, oncall-friendly way to disable compaction immediately (or gate it to a safe minimum) without requiring a full runtime restart, while preserving allocator invariants and avoiding relocation-in-progress hazards.

This PR adds the config/feature-flag plumbing and a safe-disable path (“kill switch”) for KV compaction + related relocation behavior. It also wires metrics/tracing so oncall can validate that the disable took effect, and links to the runtime runbook + rollout guardrails.

What changed: (1) Introduced runtime config keys under `kv.compaction.*` with sane defaults for release/runtime-1.22: `kv.compaction.enabled` (bool), `kv.compaction.kill_switch` (bool, higher priority than enabled), `kv.compaction.budget_ms_per_tick` (int), `kv.compaction.budget_bytes_per_tick` (int), `...

title: Console backend: add internal admin hooks to view tier limits + recent overload events
preview: Context: As part of hot-route capacity protection rollout, on-call/SRE and platform operators need a quick way (from Console) to answer two recurring questions during incidents and enterprise escalations: (1) what tier limits/budgets are currently configured for a given route group and region, and (2) have we recent...
text:
Context: As part of hot-route capacity protection rollout, on-call/SRE and platform operators need a quick way (from Console) to answer two recurring questions during incidents and enterprise escalations: (1) what tier limits/budgets are currently configured for a given route group and region, and (2) have we recently activated overload protection (admission control / shedding) and why. This PR adds backend-only hooks for an internal operator view (not exposed in public UI yet) and keeps the data/authorization boundaries tight.

What’s in this PR: Adds internal admin GraphQL resolvers and service-layer functions to (a) fetch the effective tier limit configuration for a route group (including defaults + per-route overrides after config migration) and (b) retrieve a bounded, low-cardinality list of recent overload events for that route group, with tier attribution and reason codes align...

title: Add hot-route protection dashboards + alert rules (enterprise tier + shed reasons)
preview: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling t...
text: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling the right tier first, and (3) what is the reason breakdown (quota vs overload vs admission control) when 429s occur. This PR adds Grafana dashboard definitions (jsonnet) and Prometheus alert rules for the new metrics emitted by gateway/routing/runtime (see redwood PRs for instrumentation and 429 reason codes). Scope: dashboards (per-route/per-tier latency + error + shed), annotations for protection activation events, and paging rules focused on enterprise degradation (not expected best-effort shedding). What’s included: (A) Dashboards: 1) Hot Route Protection – Overview: req...

title: Rollout eu-west protected routes and tune overload thresholds
preview: Context: We enabled hot-route capacity protection in us-east canary and validated that enterprise budgets remain stable under best-effort pressure. This PR extends the same protection to eu-west and tunes overload thresholds based on eu-west observed traffic (higher long-prompt share on /v1/chat/completions and more...
text:
Context: We enabled hot-route capacity protection in us-east canary and validated that enterprise budgets remain stable under best-effort pressure. This PR extends the same protection to eu-west and tunes overload thresholds based on eu-west observed traffic (higher long-prompt share on /v1/chat/completions and more bursty embeddings spikes in the morning UTC window). The primary goal is to preserve enterprise SLO behavior without inducing unnecessary best-effort shedding or paging noise.

What this PR does: (1) Enables the eu-west protected route group behind the region-scoped flag hot_route_protection.enabled and wires the eu-west fleet signal feed into the routing admission controller. (2) Tunes eu-west admission thresholds (queue depth + scheduler lag) using percentile distributions captured over the last 7 days; in eu-west the same absolute queue-depth thresholds caused premature...

title: Post-launch cleanup: remove legacy route throttle path
preview: Context: After GA enablement of tier-aware rate limiting/quota enforcement + priority routing for protected routes, we now have two overlapping throttling code paths in the API gateway: (1) the new PolicyEngine-backed tier-aware enforcement (route groups + priority classes + standardized 429 semantics) and (2) a leg...
text:
Context: After GA enablement of tier-aware rate limiting/quota enforcement + priority routing for protected routes, we now have two overlapping throttling code paths in the API gateway: (1) the new PolicyEngine-backed tier-aware enforcement (route groups + priority classes + standardized 429 semantics) and (2) a legacy per-route throttle path that predates hot-route protection. Keeping both paths has caused operational confusion (which one triggered?) and maintenance risk (duplicate config, inconsistent headers, and drift in retry semantics). This PR removes the legacy throttle path and consolidates all enforcement to the new tier-aware enforcement, while keeping compatibility shims for the few remaining callers/config entries.

What changed: Remove legacy throttle middleware and its config plumbing; delete deprecated route-level throttle config keys and migration fallback logic; cons...

title: Add synthetic canary for graceful degradation path (model/region fallback)
preview: Summary: This adds a new synthetic canary test that exercises Redwood’s graceful degradation behavior (fallback model and/or fallback region) and emits low-cardinality signals that map directly to incident taxonomy categories (Routing/Fallback misbehavior, Capacity/Fleet shortage, Control plane/routing config). Moti...
text: Summary: This adds a new synthetic canary test that exercises Redwood’s graceful degradation behavior (fallback model and/or fallback region) and emits low-cardinality signals that map directly to incident taxonomy categories (Routing/Fallback misbehavior, Capacity/Fleet shortage, Control plane/routing config). Motivation: Recent incidents and taxonomy workshops highlighted that we don’t currently have a reliable, continuous signal that the “degrade gracefully” path is actually working end-to-end (router decision -> fallback selection -> request succeeds -> correct fallback_reason emitted). This canary is intentionally not a throughput/perf benchmark; it is a correctness + reliability smoke test with a small number of requests on a fixed cadence. What it does: (1) Sends a small chat completion request using a policy that prefers a primary model/region, but is allowed to fall back to a...

title: Reduce cardinality for incident_type metric label (allowlist + sampling + unknown buckets)
preview: Context: PR #1459 introduced incident taxonomy dashboards and alert rules. During rollout review, Observability flagged that naively labeling high-volume request/edge metrics with incident_type can explode series cardinality (especially when incident-bot classification is missing/late, or when teams attempt to encod...
text: Context: PR #1459 introduced incident taxonomy dashboards and alert rules. During rollout review, Observability flagged that naively labeling high-volume request/edge metrics with incident_type can explode series cardinality (especially when incident-bot classification is missing/late, or when teams attempt to encode subtypes/notes). This follow-up tightens the metric labeling contract and adjusts recording rules to keep the dashboards useful without putting Prometheus/Mimir under stress. What changed: 1) Added an explicit allowlist for incident_type values used in metrics (top-level types only, no freeform). 2) Introduced two stable buckets for missing/invalid classification: incident_type="unknown" (not set) and incident_type="unclassified" (set but not in allowlist / failed validation). 3) Moved incident_type labeling off the highest-cardinality per-route/per-model series and onto ...

title: Add region + hardware SKU dimension config (with fanout safeguards)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds first-class configuration for running perf-canary across multiple **regions** and **hardware SKUs**, while ensuring we don’t accidentally fan out to every region/SKU combination (and burn the overhead budget). The canary runner now supports: (1) Target selection by `region` and `hardware_sku`...
text:
## Summary This PR adds first-class configuration for running perf-canary across multiple **regions** and **hardware SKUs**, while ensuring we don’t accidentally fan out to every region/SKU combination (and burn the overhead budget). The canary runner now supports: (1) Target selection by `region` and `hardware_sku` (optional), (2) A hard fanout guard that defaults to conservative behavior, and (3) Safer defaults for Helm/Env-based deployments. This is needed for Phase 1 rollout where we want to start in `us-east` on a single SKU, then expand gradually to `eu-west` and additional SKUs once baselines/thresholds are tuned.

## Motivation / Context Today the canary targets are effectively “global” (implicitly whatever the control plane routes to). That makes it hard to: - Attribute regressions to fleet changes (SKU-specific) vs runtime changes - Set region-specific baselines (telemetry v...

title: Add failover summary events to incident timeline for auto-populated postmortem milestones
preview:
## Context
During the 2026-01-15 controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west), we had to manually reconstruct the key milestones (trigger time, policy flip, drain start, rollback, steady-state) across multiple sources (routing audit logs, controller logs, Slack timeline notes). This PR integrates failover automa...
text:
## Context
During the 2026-01-15 controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west), we had to manually reconstruct the key milestones (trigger time, policy flip, drain start, rollback, steady-state) across multiple sources (routing audit logs, controller logs, Slack timeline notes). This PR integrates failover automation events into the incident timeline tooling so that postmortems can auto-populate those milestones and on-call can see a consistent view during an event.

This is part of the Regional Failover Automation Upgrade program and complements the new structured failover audit events (recommendation/execution/rollback) emitted by routing/control plane.

## What this changes
- Adds a new incident-timeline ingest adapter for `failover.audit_event` records emitted by the failover controller/policy engine.
- Normalizes audit events into timeline entries with stable, low-cardinality ...

title: Expose incident taxonomy fields in Ops Console API (type/subtype/owner) with RBAC checks
preview: Context: As part of the incident taxonomy + ownership mapping rollout, Console (ops-only today; CS read-only later) needs to display the incident classification consistently with incident-bot and dashboards. Today Console pulls incident summaries from the internal ops API, but the response doesn’t include incident_t...
text:
Context: As part of the incident taxonomy + ownership mapping rollout, Console (ops-only today; CS read-only later) needs to display the incident classification consistently with incident-bot and dashboards. Today Console pulls incident summaries from the internal ops API, but the response doesn’t include incident_type/subtype or the resolved owning team, which forces folks to cross-reference Slack or postmortems and leads to mismatches during live incidents.

This PR extends the internal Ops Console API to return:
- incident_type (top-level taxonomy key)
- incident_subtype (optional; taxonomy subtype key)
- owner_team (resolved primary owner, derived from type/subtype mapping)
- owner_resolution_source ("explicit" | "mapping" | "unknown") to make it clear whether the incident commander set an owner vs it was inferred

Permissioning:
- Adds an RBAC-gated read path: callers must have o...

title: Failover controller: rate-limit automation actions + retries to prevent oscillation under noisy signals
preview: Context: During staging rehearsals we observed oscillation risk in the failover controller when region health inputs get noisy (partial reachability loss, intermittent 5xx spikes, and delayed capacity signals). While ADR-043 + the anti-flap timers in PR-19815 reduce flip/flop behavior, we still had two failure modes...
text:
Context: During staging rehearsals we observed oscillation risk in the failover controller when region health inputs get noisy (partial reachability loss, intermittent 5xx spikes, and delayed capacity signals). While ADR-043 + the anti-flap timers in PR-19815 reduce flip/flop behavior, we still had two failure modes: (1) the controller repeatedly re-attempts the same action (e.g., re-apply a policy flip) when the policy engine or routing API is degraded, and (2) the controller makes too many distinct automation actions across tenants/routes in a short window, amplifying load and making rollback reasoning harder.

This PR introduces explicit rate limiting for automation actions and retries, with metrics + structured logs so on-call can see when guardrails are gating behavior.

What changed (high level): - Added an in-controller rate limiter for action execution and for retry loops. - E...

title: Add fallback policy guardrails for enterprise protected routes
preview: Context: As part of hot-route capacity protection, routing is now capable of using capacity signals to (a) admit/deny best-effort traffic earlier and (b) optionally fall back to compatible model variants/regions under pressure. For enterprise protected routes, we need explicit guardrails so we do not silently change...
text:
Context: As part of hot-route capacity protection, routing is now capable of using capacity signals to (a) admit/deny best-effort traffic earlier and (b) optionally fall back to compatible model variants/regions under pressure. For enterprise protected routes, we need explicit guardrails so we do not silently change model/region behavior for high-SLA workloads, and so on-call can explain decisions deterministically during escalations.

What this PR does: 1) Introduces explicit fallback policy for protected enterprise routes via route-group config + policy-engine evaluation output. Default behavior for enterprise priority is strict admission control only (no fallback) unless the route group is explicitly marked as fallback-eligible. 2) Adds separate toggles for model-variant fallback vs region fallback, and enforces compliance boundary constraints for region fallback (no cross-boundary...

title: Add failover safety limits per tenant (max flips/hour + blast-radius constraints)
preview: Context: As we enable automated regional failover + rollback, we need hard guardrails to prevent a single noisy signal (or misconfig) from causing repeated policy flips or a large blast-radius for any one tenant. This PR adds per-tenant safety limits enforced by the failover controller and surfaced via structured lo...
text:
Context: As we enable automated regional failover + rollback, we need hard guardrails to prevent a single noisy signal (or misconfig) from causing repeated policy flips or a large blast-radius for any one tenant. This PR adds per-tenant safety limits enforced by the failover controller and surfaced via structured logs/audit events for on-call visibility.

What this changes: 1) Per-tenant action rate limits: enforce max_policy_flips_per_hour (sliding window) at tenant_id scope, with optional overrides per tenant tier (free/pro/enterprise) and per route group. 2) Blast-radius constraints: prevent automated actions that would affect more than max_regions_affected or max_models_affected in a single evaluation cycle for a tenant (e.g., avoid flipping all model routes at once). 3) Enforcement visibility: when a limit blocks an action, we emit (a) a structured enforcement log entry with evid...

title: Dedicated drain: add progress metrics + dashboard hooks
preview: Context: As part of the regional failover automation upgrade, we added a Dedicated pool drain workflow (PR #19822) but on-call has had limited visibility into whether a drain is making forward progress vs stuck (and how close we are to a safe cutover). This PR adds drain progress metrics emitted by the drain coordin...
text: Context: As part of the regional failover automation upgrade, we added a Dedicated pool drain workflow (PR #19822) but on-call has had limited visibility into whether a drain is making forward progress vs stuck (and how close we are to a safe cutover). This PR adds drain progress metrics emitted by the drain coordinator and wires those metrics into the existing failover signal dashboards so the game day runbook can validate drain progress in real time. What’s included: 1) New metrics for Dedicated drain lifecycle and progress, emitted from the dedicated-drain coordinator and keyed by pool_id (bounded) + region + customer_tier. 2) A periodic progress sampler that computes remaining nodes/replicas, in-flight request counts, and an ETA based on recent completion rate; sampler is explicitly best-effort and will not block drain execution. 3) An annotation event emitted when a drain starts/...

title: Add incident owner mapping config to control plane (versioned + validated + audited)
preview: Context: As part of the Reliability Program (incident taxonomy + ownership mapping), we need a single source of truth for incident owner resolution that is (1) versioned and reviewable, (2) validated to avoid typos / ambiguous matches, (3) auditable (who changed what + when), and (4) safe to roll forward/back withou...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program (incident taxonomy + ownership mapping), we need a single source of truth for incident owner resolution that is (1) versioned and reviewable, (2) validated to avoid typos / ambiguous matches, (3) auditable (who changed what + when), and (4) safe to roll forward/back without paging the wrong teams mid-incident. This PR introduces a control-plane managed config for mapping incident_type + subtype -> owning team metadata, plus guardrails and rollout tooling.

What this PR does: Adds a new control-plane config kind `incident_owner_mapping.v1` stored and served by the config service. The config maps {incident_type, incident_subtype} to: primary_owner_team, secondary_owner_team (optional), consulted_teams (optional), escalation_channel, and runbook_links (optional). Adds server-side validation (schema + allowlists + uniqueness + cardinality checks...

title: Auto-suggest incident_type from symptoms (5xx/latency/streaming/capacity) with override
preview: Context: As part of the Reliability Program "Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping" we’re making incident_type/subtype required in incident-bot. This PR adds lightweight heuristics to auto-suggest a likely incident type based on the initial symptoms so we reduce time-to-classify during live incidents while keeping t...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program "Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping" we’re making incident_type/subtype required in incident-bot. This PR adds lightweight heuristics to auto-suggest a likely incident type based on the initial symptoms so we reduce time-to-classify during live incidents while keeping the human as the source of truth.

What’s in this PR: 1) Suggestion engine: Adds a new suggestion module that generates a (type, subtype, confidence, rationale) suggestion from the incident creation context. Inputs include: incident-bot symptom selections (5xx spike, p95 latency regression, streaming disconnects/resets, capacity/fleet alerts), any attached alert payload (title + common labels), and a small set of structured hints we already capture (tier, region, affected surface: hosted/dedicated/private). The output is always constrained to the v1 taxonomy allowlist; if we...

title: Failover controller: add optional approval hooks before executing automation
preview: Context: As part of the Regional Failover Automation Upgrade, we want the controller to be able to run in production with automation enabled while still requiring an explicit human ack (on-call) before it flips routing policies for certain tiers/routes. Today the controller either executes immediately (when enabled)...
text:
Context: As part of the Regional Failover Automation Upgrade, we want the controller to be able to run in production with automation enabled while still requiring an explicit human ack (on-call) before it flips routing policies for certain tiers/routes. Today the controller either executes immediately (when enabled) or runs dry-run only; there is no first-class “pending approval” state, so teams have been relying on manual toggles/feature flags during game days and incidents.

What this PR does: Adds an optional approval gate to the failover controller execution path. When configured, the controller will (1) compute the recommended actions as usual, (2) emit an audit event with status=PENDING_APPROVAL containing the evidence signals and the exact policy changes, and (3) pause execution until it receives an approval decision (approve/deny) or times out. The gate is per-environment and ...

title: Hotfix: prevent failover controller NPE when a signal stream is missing
preview: Context: During staging rehearsal for regional failover automation, the failover controller crashed with a nil pointer dereference when one of the signal streams (capacity/headroom) was absent for a subset of regions/routes. This can happen during partial outages of the signal publisher, misconfigured routing config...
text:
Context: During staging rehearsal for regional failover automation, the failover controller crashed with a nil pointer dereference when one of the signal streams (capacity/headroom) was absent for a subset of regions/routes. This can happen during partial outages of the signal publisher, misconfigured routing configs in staging, or when a region is newly added and the signal pipeline hasn’t started publishing yet. The controller should degrade safely (no action + clear evidence) rather than crash-loop.

What changed: 1) Make signal reads total: convert all signal fetch paths in the controller to return an explicit tri-state (present/absent/stale) instead of a nil map/struct. Missing signals no longer cause an NPE. 2) Deterministic behavior on missing inputs: if a signal required for corroboration (per ADR-042) is missing, the controller marks the evaluation as `insufficient_evidence` ...

title: Dedicated: add internal pool drain endpoint + guarded workflow for regional failover
preview: Context: As part of the Regional Failover Automation Upgrade (ENG-2411), we need a safe, deterministic way to drain Dedicated GPU pools during regional impairment and controlled failovers. Today, draining is a manual sequence across fleet tooling + routing toggles, which is slow and error-prone during an incident. T...
text:
Context: As part of the Regional Failover Automation Upgrade (ENG-2411), we need a safe, deterministic way to drain Dedicated GPU pools during regional impairment and controlled failovers. Today, draining is a manual sequence across fleet tooling + routing toggles, which is slow and error-prone during an incident. This PR adds an internal endpoint and workflow to request/observe a drain, with explicit safety gates and auditability.

What’s in this PR: 1) New internal control-plane endpoint: POST /internal/dedicated/pools/{pool_id}/drain (and GET /internal/dedicated/pools/{pool_id}/drain/{drain_id}) that initiates a drain request and returns a drain_id for tracking. Requests are idempotent via Idempotency-Key and constrained by RBAC (internal operator + automation controller only). 2) Drain workflow/state machine: QUEUED -> VALIDATING -> DRAINING -> WAITING_FOR_ZERO -> COMPLETE (or FAI...
links: Linear ENG-2420: Dedicated pool drain automation
Linear ENG-2411: Regional failover automation upgrade

title: Add integration tests for tiered quota + priority routing (quota windows, fallback vs shedding, concurrent load)
preview:
## Summary
Adds a focused integration test suite validating the new hot-route capacity protection behavior end-to-end across: API Gateway (tier-aware rate limiting + quota headers), Policy Engine (priority class + deterministic decisions), and Routing (admission control + fallback guardrails).

This PR covers three ...
text:
## Summary
Adds a focused integration test suite validating the new hot-route capacity protection behavior end-to-end across: API Gateway (tier-aware rate limiting + quota headers), Policy Engine (priority class + deterministic decisions), and Routing (admission control + fallback guardrails).

This PR covers three high-risk areas called out in the rollout plan:
1) quota windows behave correctly and are observable (429 semantics/headers are stable)
2) priority routing behavior is correct under pressure (enterprise budget preserved; best-effort throttled first)
3) correctness under concurrency (no racy accounting; decisions stay deterministic under load)

## Why
We had good unit coverage per component, but gaps showed up during canary when multiple signals interact (quota vs overload vs fallback). We need a single harness that spins up gateway+routing+policy with predictable time + pre...

title: Hotfix: fix metric cardinality regression in overload events
preview: Context: During the hot-route capacity protection rollout we introduced new overload event metrics (shed/admission_control) that were intended to be low-cardinality (route_group, tier, region, reason). In the last merge window we accidentally started emitting per-request / per-tenant labels on the overload event cou...
text: Context: During the hot-route capacity protection rollout we introduced new overload event metrics (shed/admission_control) that were intended to be low-cardinality (route_group, tier, region, reason). In the last merge window we accidentally started emitting per-request / per-tenant labels on the overload event counters due to a tag plumbing change in the gateway -> telemetry bridge. This caused a rapid increase in series count in Prometheus and intermittent ingestion backpressure, and it also made the SRE dashboards noisy/slow to load. What changed: (1) Add a strict label allowlist for overload event metrics in the telemetry emitter and drop/ignore all non-allowed labels (e.g., tenant_id, request_id, trace_id, api_key_id, user_agent, model_revision). (2) Split the old overloaded "overload_event" into two metrics: redwood_overload_shed_total and redwood_overload_admission_denied_tota...

title: Post overload-protection activations to #incidents with dashboards + runbook links
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a new incident-bot event handler for Hot-route Capacity Protection activation events emitted by routing/gateway. When protection enters an active state (or exits), incident-bot posts a structured Slack message to `#incidents` with:
- route_group + route (when available), region, tier class, a...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a new incident-bot event handler for Hot-route Capacity Protection activation events emitted by routing/gateway. When protection enters an active state (or exits), incident-bot posts a structured Slack message to `#incidents` with:
- route_group + route (when available), region, tier class, and activation type
- primary reason code(s) (quota vs overload) and protection mode (admission control vs shedding)
- current/peak metrics snapshot (shed_rate, 429_rate, p95 latency, queue depth where present)
- links to the SRE dashboard + runbook section
- a dedupe key to avoid paging storms during flappy thresholds

This is intended to reduce time-to-diagnosis during overload events and make it obvious when best-effort is being throttled to preserve enterprise SLOs.

## Context
Hot-route capacity protection is rolling out behind flags (see ENG-4927). During the us-east c...

title: Require incident_type/subtype on create; auto-resolve owner + escalation channel
preview:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program “Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”, we’re making incident_type a required field at incident creation time, and we want the bot to resolve an owner team consistently (single primary owner) to reduce time-to-engage and improve reporting quality.

This PR updates the Sla...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program “Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”, we’re making incident_type a required field at incident creation time, and we want the bot to resolve an owner team consistently (single primary owner) to reduce time-to-engage and improve reporting quality.

This PR updates the Slack modal + slash command flow to require incident_type and incident_subtype (from the v1 allowlist), then performs owner resolution (type/subtype -> primary owner + escalation channel) and posts the result into the incident channel. The goal is: (1) classification is required and auditable, (2) owner is consistently derived from a versioned mapping, and (3) the IC gets an immediate “who owns this” + “where to escalate” suggestion.

User-facing changes:
- /incident create now blocks submission until incident_type is selected.
- incident_subtype is required when the type has ...
